title: File descriptor resource type — boxed term, refcounted FD lifecycle
purpose: BEAM represents open file descriptors and sockets as port terms. In beamr's completion-based IO model, we need a lighter-weight resource type: a boxed term on the process heap that holds a raw file descriptor with reference-counted lifecycle management. When the owning process exits or the resource is garbage collected, the FD is closed automatically. This is the foundation for all file and socket operations — every BIF in B-103 through B-111 operates on FdResource terms. The resource type also tracks the owning process (for controlling-process semantics in TCP/UDP) and the current IO state (open, closing, closed).
task: Define an FdResource boxed term type with a new BoxedTag. Implement reference counting for FD lifecycle (FDs are shared between the heap term and the ring's in-flight operations). Add GC integration so unreferenced FdResources close their FDs. Register the resource type in the boxed term system.

requirements.title: FdResource boxed term type
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an FdResource heap layout with a new BoxedTag::FdResource. THE SYSTEM SHALL store the raw file descriptor, owning process PID, resource state, and a shared reference to the FD lifecycle manager.
acceptance: BoxedTag::FdResource = 0x1B added to the BoxedTag enum
FdResource heap layout: [header(tag=0x19, size), fd_ptr] where fd_ptr is a pointer to an Arc<FdInner>
FdInner struct: { fd: RawFd, owner_pid: u64, state: AtomicU8 (Open=0, Closing=1, Closed=2) }
write_fd_resource(heap, fd_inner: Arc<FdInner>) -> Option<Term> allocates on process heap
FdResource accessor: FdResource::new(term: Term) -> Option<Self> validates tag, provides fd(), owner_pid(), state(), inner() methods
Arc<FdInner> ensures the FD stays alive while any reference exists (heap term, in-flight ring ops, etc.)
Test: write an FdResource to the heap, read it back, verify fd and owner

requirements.title: FD lifecycle management — close on drop
requirements.spec: WHEN the last Arc<FdInner> reference is dropped, THE SYSTEM SHALL close the raw file descriptor via the CompletionRing (async close) or libc::close (synchronous fallback).
acceptance: FdInner implements Drop: transitions state to Closing, submits Close op to ring if available, else calls libc::close
Double-close protection: state check prevents closing an already-closed FD
explicit_close(ring: &dyn CompletionRing) method for BIF-initiated close — submits async close and transitions state
Test: create FdResource, drop all references, verify FD is closed (try read on closed FD fails)
Test: explicit close followed by drop does not double-close

requirements.title: GC integration for FdResource
requirements.spec: WHEN the garbage collector encounters a BoxedTag::FdResource during heap traversal, THE SYSTEM SHALL copy the header and fd_ptr words, and clone the Arc<FdInner> to maintain the reference count.
acceptance: GC's rewrite_copied_object handles BoxedTag::FdResource: copies 2 words (header + fd_ptr)
Arc clone incremented during copy so the FdInner is not prematurely dropped
When the old heap is freed, the old FdResource's Arc reference is decremented
FdResource terms survive minor and major GC correctly
Test: allocate FdResource, trigger GC, verify FD is still open and accessible

requirements.title: Process exit closes owned FDs
requirements.spec: WHEN a process exits, THE SYSTEM SHALL close all FdResource terms owned by that process by iterating the process heap and closing any FdInner with matching owner_pid.
acceptance: Process cleanup path (in scheduler exit handling) scans for FdResource terms on the heap
Each FdResource with owner_pid matching the exiting process is explicitly closed
FDs with other owners (shared resources) are not closed — only the Arc reference is dropped
Test: process opens a file, process exits, verify FD is closed

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement specific file or socket operations — those are B-103 through B-111
SHALL NOT implement controlling-process transfer — that is part of B-108
SHALL NOT implement port compatibility — FdResource is a new type, not an emulation of BEAM ports
SHALL NOT use finalizers or weak references — Arc Drop is the lifecycle mechanism
verification: cargo test passes with FdResource allocation and GC
FD is closed exactly once when all references are dropped
GC correctly copies FdResource terms without FD leaks
Process exit closes all owned FDs
FdResource round-trips through minor and major GC
